text: The surgeon begins a total laparoscopic hysterectomy by cutting the right round ligament using a ligature device. The bladder appears slightly adhered due to previous surgeries. The ovarian ligament is identified, and care is taken to stay close to the ovary to prevent bleeding, as the ovary will be preserved. The major ovarian ligament and remnants of the major salpings are separated on the left side. The remaining portion of the right round ligament is cut before proceeding to the left side.

The surgeon uses a Robi Dissector in the left hand and a blunt tip Maryland with a 37 cm Ligasure device to manipulate the ovarian ligament on the left side. The UV fold is opened using a harmonic device, while the bulky cervix of the uterus limits traction due to the colpotomizer not fitting properly.

The surgeon opens the right anterior leaf of the broad ligament and begins separating the peritoneum of the uterovesical fold. Adhesions between the bladder and surrounding tissues, due to previous caesarean sections, are carefully separated using the blunt tip of a suction device.

The surgeon separates the posterior peritoneum on both the left and right sides, taking care to avoid the vein with the harmonic tip to prevent bleeding. The peritoneum over the cervical part of the uterosacral ligament is separated. The vascular bundle on the left side is coagulated twice using the ligature device, which is held in the left hand. On the right side, the ligature is positioned to point towards the vagina for coagulation and cutting.

The uterus appears pale as anterior colpotomy is initiated using the vibrating tip of the harmonic device, which is applied to the anterior, left lateral, and right lateral aspects. The uterus is pulled out, with the remaining part of the cervix being separated. The surgeon approximates the angle by taking a bite on the uterosacral ligament of the right side and feeds the suture through the anterior vaginal wall, preparing to use an extracorporeal square knot.

The surgeon takes a bite at the left side of the uterosacral ligament angle and the anterior vaginal wall, pushing the bladder upwards. Full thickness of the epithelium and fascia is taken together, followed by the application of an extracorporeal square knot using a Clark knot pusher. The vault is approximated and closed tightly to prevent collapse. The assistant holds the suture while suction irrigation is performed to check for active bleeding, confirming the fluid is clear.
